title: I sent $50K. My supplier got $48,500. Here is why.

scripts.subtitle: Real talk + escalating personal energy
scripts.original_vo: Real talk for every business owner sourcing from China. You think your bank is helping you. They're not. You wire $50,000 to your supplier. They get $48,000. You call the bank. They blame the intermediary. The intermediary blames the receiving bank. Nobody takes responsibility. Meanwhile your supplier won't start production. You're losing days. Your customers are waiting. Your margin is bleeding. And you're on the phone with a teller who has no idea what you're talking about. I see this every week. Every single week. Stop. WorldFirst. Near-interbank rate. Same-day to Chinese accounts. Full $50,000 lands. Supplier starts. You sleep. Search WorldFirst. Now.

scripts.iterated_vo_clarity: This one is for every business owner who sources from China. Picture this. You wire 50,000 dollars to your supplier. A few days later, they call. They only got 48,000. You think, no problem, I will sort it. You call your bank. They look at the wire. They say it must be the bank in the middle. You call that bank. They say it must be the receiving bank in China. The receiving bank says it must be the rate your bank used. Nobody takes the blame. Meanwhile, your supplier is sitting on their hands. They will not start production until the full amount arrives. Days go by. Your customers are emailing you about their order. Your margin is shrinking. And you are still on the phone with a bank teller who has no real answer. We see this story play out again and again. We built WorldFirst so you do not have to live it. We give you the near-interbank rate, the real one. The full 50,000 lands in your supplier's Chinese account same day. Your supplier starts production. You sleep. Search WorldFirst.

PG5.analysis: Cleanest script in the set. The 'call the bank, they blame intermediary, intermediary blames receiving' beat is a real and well-known pain point that any importer who's been stuck on that call will immediately recognize as true. Soft on specific numbers (no 2.5%, no 1,200, no compound math). 'Your margin is bleeding' is metaphor not claim. 'This happens every week. Every single week.' is the only overreach. Most defensible script.

skeptic_comments.text: Ya the blame game is real. Bank, intermediary, receiving all pointing finger.
Every week? Bit much. Maybe every few months.
Supplier starts and I sleep, nice line but show me how WorldFirst do it lah.
Who is WorldFirst? Got Malaysia license?
Ok this one feel less ad than others. Still want proof.
